data.cheatsheetContent:
You are an expert in universal algebra. Decide whether Equation 1 implies Equation 2 over all magmas.

A MAGMA is a set M with a single binary operation * (no axioms: NOT associative, commutative, or with identity). All variables universally quantified.

"Equation 1 implies Equation 2" means: every magma satisfying Equation 1 also satisfies Equation 2.

Equation 1: {{ equation1 }}
Equation 2: {{ equation2 }}

---

STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: associativity, commutativity, or any property not stated in Equation 1.

REWRITING RULE: Eq 1 has the form L = R. To apply it to subterm T:
  • Forward (L→R): consistent σ with σ(L)=T; result σ(R).
  • Backward (R→L): consistent σ with σ(R)=T; result σ(L).
  • CRITICAL: When L is "x*(…)", A*B matches only if A = σ(x) exactly — not a compound term containing x.

---

STEP 1 — Try to DERIVE Equation 2 from Equation 1 (≤3 steps).
If Equation 2 has a variable NOT in Equation 1, skip to Step 2.

STEP 2 — Counterexample search.

Try these families. For each, verify eq1 holds, then test eq2.

  Family A: a*b = a. Substitute every (P*Q) → P.
  Family B: a*b = b. Substitute every (P*Q) → Q.
  Family C: a*b = f(a), 3-cycle 0→1→2→0. Substitute (P*Q) → f(P). Use f(f(f(x)))=x.
  Family D: a*b = f(b), same f. Substitute (P*Q) → f(Q). Use f(f(f(x)))=x.
  Family E: M={0,1,2}: 0*0=0, 0*1=0, 0*2=1, 1*k=1, 2*k=2 (all k). Check eq1 for all inputs; if holds, test eq2.

A counterexample requires: eq1 holds for ALL inputs AND eq2 fails for SOME input (show numbers).
If a family fails eq1 for any input, skip it.

STOP RULE: After all 5 families — if no verified counterexample found → \boxed{TRUE} immediately. Do not test other magmas.

---

REASONING BUDGET: ≤12 lines. Do not restate equations.

OUTPUT FORMAT:

Write your reasoning (derivation steps or family checks), then on the FINAL LINE only:
\boxed{TRUE}    or    \boxed{FALSE}

CRITICAL: \boxed{} appears EXACTLY ONCE — as the last non-empty line.
